description: Three genuine Windows source bugs hid behind 'test path-literal' noise during the CI-3c 93-failure sweep
resolution: Triage each Windows failure test-vs-source, never blanket-toPosix. The 3 real source bugs + fixes: (1) src/core/atomic-write.ts .bak prune re-sorted filenames lexically, diverging from manifest insertion order when hrtime tail wraps a 1e9-ns boundary -> delete the dropped manifest entries directly (insertion order). (2) src/adapters/{copilot,roo-code}/session.ts dedupeByRealpath didn't case-fold -> double-counted on case-insensitive NTFS/APFS; case-fold the dedup key on win32/darwin (no-op on Linux). (3) src/wiki/resolve.ts assertPathInside hardcoded sep='/' -> rejected every in-tree file on Windows where resolve() emits backslash; use path.sep. Verified Linux-equivalent + full suite 3312 pass integrated.

description: process.env.X = undefined is a cross-platform footgun in BOTH test and product code
resolution: On POSIX Bun, 'process.env.X = undefined' deletes the var; on Windows it coerces to the literal STRING 'undefined' (truthy). Because bun test runs all files in ONE shared process AND integration tests spawn subprocesses with env:{...process.env}, a single bad assignment poisons sibling files and child processes. Symptom can surface far away (e.g. AM_ENCRYPTION_KEY='undefined' -> importKey -> atob('undefined') throws 'The string contains invalid characters', which apply.ts's catch-all then masks as 'Config not found'). ALWAYS use Reflect.deleteProperty(process.env, 'X') (or delete). Found in tests (config, secrets-backend-selection) AND product code (pair-accept.ts:245 finally block). grep repo-wide for 'process.env.[A-Z_]* = undefined'.
